title: BuildKit Graphs, Cache, Multi-Stage Builds, Mounts, and Secret Boundaries · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Build Mounts Secrets Ssh
summary: A CI build is slow, leaks credentials through configuration, and ships compilers that production never needs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker build mounts secrets ssh increment in cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use bind, cache, tmpfs, secret, and SSH RUN mounts with scoped lifetime, ownership, and cache behavior.
Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.

version-baseline:

  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

theory case — A theory BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence. Competency probe: Use bind, cache, tmpfs, secret, and SSH RUN mounts with scoped lifetime, ownership, and cache behavior. A defensible solution applies Docker Build Mounts Secrets Ssh, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-4
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: worked docker buildkit cache multistage 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Build arguments and ENV are safe ways to pass credentials because later RUN steps can unset them. This fails the Docker Build Mounts Secrets Ssh contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
